compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DERISK (E4, local Docker on arm64): official gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 boots cleanly on arm64 and serves REST (version '3.14.1'). Bare minor tags (3.14/3.13) DO NOT exist on Docker Hub — pin a patch tag (3.14.1/3.13.7/3.12.8; all publish arm64). The %2F-safe nginx reverse-proxy (proxy_pass with NO path, + an /mcp/ route for the MCP) passes 'nginx -t'. Operational note: keep a clear host-port plan (a co-tenant Keycloak on 8080 blocked an initial bind — trivial, but pin ports).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S2 implemented + deployed LIVE on i-00880b2c7f13527c5 — 2026-06-30 (commit 6cd6f4e1f)

**Stack:** docker-compose runs gerrit (gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1, arm64) + the review-bot (FastAPI). Host nginx terminates TLS and proxies; host certbot manages the cert.

**AC verification (all live-verified):**
- Gerrit reachable at https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/ with a valid Let's Encrypt cert (issuer Let's Encrypt, curl ssl_verify_result=0); `/config/server/version` -> 200 "3.14.1".
- %2F round-trip: a request to `/projects/test%2Fencoded` appears in Gerrit httpd_log as `test%2Fencoded` (NOT decoded to `test/encoded`) — nginx bare `proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8080;` preserves the encoded slash end-to-end.
- certbot renewal automated: systemd `certbot-renew.timer` enabled (twice daily, nginx-reload deploy-hook); `certbot renew --dry-run` -> "Congratulations, all simulated renewals succeeded". nginx :80 serves /.well-known/acme-challenge/.
- Gerrit SSH on 29418 works: `ssh -p29418 admin@host gerrit version` -> 3.14.1; `git clone ssh://admin@host:29418/All-Projects` succeeds. Admin (account 1000000, Administrators) bootstrapped; SSH key registered via NoteDb (refs/users/00/1000000 authorized_keys).
- Data persists across `docker compose down`/`up` AND an instance reboot: stateful site subdirs are bind-mounted from the EBS data volume (/var/gerrit/site/{git,index,cache,db,etc,logs,plugins}); the admin account + SSH key survived multiple down/up cycles (re-cloned successfully). Bind mounts on the EBS volume are inherently safe from `docker compose down -v` (which only removes named volumes).
- review-bot reachable through nginx at /review/: GET /review/health -> 200 {"status":"ok"}; POST /review/webhook -> 202 (reaches the receiver process, not a 502).
- Secrets from SSM via fetch-secrets.sh into a 0600, gitignored, ephemeral .env (instance role, IMDSv2, fail-fast on unreachable/empty/CHANGEME). LLM path enabled by ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + [agents]; REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM NOT set. **.dockerignore added** so the .env/.git never bake into the image layer (verified absent in the built image).
- IAM SSM read scoped to parameter/rebar/prod/* (S1; S2 adds no extra IAM — iam_s2.tf is a documentation anchor).
- Both containers log to journald (verified `logging.driver: journald`); health probe (/, /review/health) via systemd timer rebar-observability.timer logging to journald; CloudWatch disk-usage alarm `rebar-gerrit-data-disk-high` (>=85% of /var/gerrit) created.
- Gerrit at an existing arm64 patch tag 3.14.1, heapLimit=2g, packedGitLimit=1g budgeted OFF-heap.

**Non-obvious fixes during live bring-up (all folded into the repo):**
- The official image runs from /var/gerrit and ignores GERRIT_SITE -> persist stateful SUBDIRS (not the whole site) so baked binaries aren't shadowed.
- gerrit.sh infinite-loops on modern JDK (no lib/tools.jar) unless container.javaHome is set; init sets it on first boot but a full-file config re-seed clobbered it -> gerrit.config is now self-sufficient (javaHome + javaOptions + basePath + serverId pinned).
- Cookie-auth REST mutations are blocked (no obtainable XSRF/HTTP password from a clean slate) -> admin SSH key registered deterministically via NoteDb.
- Gerrit proxy redirect-loop avoided via Host $host + X-Forwarded-Proto https + proxy-https listenUrl + matching canonicalWebUrl.

**Code review (subagent):** 2 blockers — missing .dockerignore (secrets baked into image) and bootstrap aborting on a fresh NoteDb user branch — both FIXED and verified (image has no .env/.git; bootstrap now dev-logins to create the account then guards the fetch). Nits (stale comment, empty-SSM guard) addressed.

Plugin install + bot identity/event wiring are S4a; receiver review/vote LOGIC is S4b (S2 ships the skeleton).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Persistence AC hardened: stateful Gerrit site subdirs are now EXTERNAL named volumes (gerrit_git/index/cache/db/etc/logs/plugins, all external: true) created by compose-up.sh via 'docker volume create' (local bind onto /var/gerrit/site/* on the EBS volume) BEFORE 'docker compose up'. 'docker compose down -v' cannot destroy them; baked binaries not shadowed (per-subdir, not whole /var/gerrit). Verified: after down/up the admin SSH clone still succeeds (data persisted).

compiled_state.description:
# S2 — Deploy Gerrit + review-bot receiver (docker-compose) behind nginx + TLS

## Why
Run Gerrit and the **review-bot webhook receiver** as the two containers on the box, reachable
over HTTPS at `rebar.solutions.navateam.com`. Gerrit needs a `%2F`-safe reverse proxy — the
community uses nginx/Apache, NOT Caddy (Caddy URL-decodes paths by default with no global
switch, which breaks Gerrit's change/project screens; no confirmed Caddy+Gerrit recipe exists).
This is the bottom layer of the d251 "proven pipe": Gerrit serving, the receiver reachable over
HTTP, TLS terminated, secrets from SSM. NOTE (peer-pattern correction): Gerrit POSTs webhook
JSON, not MCP protocol — so the right component is a thin HTTP **webhook receiver that IMPORTS
the review kernel as a library**, NOT the stdio rebar-MCP server exposed over HTTP (a FastMCP
HTTP transport would reject a Gerrit webhook by spec; `streamable-http` is a red herring here).

## What
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`:
  - official `gerritcodereview/gerrit` pinned to a PATCH tag (e.g. `3.14.1`) with persistent
    volumes (git/index/cache/etc/db on the data volume) + the two-step init. DERISK NOTE (E4):
    bare minor tags `3.14`/`3.13` do NOT exist on Docker Hub; pin a published patch tag
    (`3.14.1`/`3.13.7`/`3.12.8`), all of which publish arm64 manifests.
  - the **review-bot container** (signing key + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY sourced from SSM into the boot
    `.env` — see secrets bullet; write-gating as needed); both `restart: unless-stopped`, pinned
    tags. The receiver calls the `rebar.llm` library functions DIRECTLY, so the LLM review path is
    enabled simply by `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` being present + the `[agents]` extra being installed —
    there is NO separate allow-flag for direct library calls (`REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` gates only the
    stdio MCP server's tool wrappers, which this receiver does not use, so it is intentionally not
    set).
  - the Gerrit data volume is declared **`external: true`** (created once, outside compose) so a
    stray `docker compose down -v` cannot destroy git/NoteDb/index state. S2 only DECLARES this
    external data volume; volume snapshotting + retention is owned by **S7's DLM daily-snapshot
    policy** (see S7), and the restore procedure is in the runbook. Freeze-and-restore (the d251
    recovery posture, no break-glass v1) operates against the snapshots S7 manages.
- **Review-bot webhook receiver (in-scope S2 deliverable).** The second container is a thin
  **webhook->review->vote receiver** (an ASGI/FastAPI service under `src/rebar/review_bot/`)
  that IMPORTS the review kernel as a library and exposes a plain HTTP endpoint Gerrit's
  `webhooks` plugin POSTs to. This is deliberately NOT the stdio rebar-MCP server over HTTP
  (Gerrit speaks webhooks, not MCP JSON-RPC; an MCP HTTP transport would reject the request).
  The stdio MCP server is unchanged and remains for agent use only. The receiver binds
  `0.0.0.0:<PORT>` (default `8000`); nginx routes
  `location /review/ { proxy_pass http://review-bot:<PORT>/; }`. Decision recorded in
  `docs/adr/0007-review-bot-receiver.md`.
- `gerrit.config`: `container.heapLimit=2g` with `core.packedGitLimit` budgeted INSIDE the heap
  (~512m-1g), NoteDb, `canonicalWebUrl=https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/`, sshd/httpd thread
  caps + `sshd.batchThreads` to avoid parallel-clone OOM.
- nginx + certbot (Let's Encrypt) terminating TLS on 443: route Gerrit (`%2F`-safe: `proxy_pass`
  with no path) and the receiver (`location /review/`) behind the one cert; Gerrit SSH on 29418.
- **certbot renewal lifecycle.** Initial issuance AND renewal: a systemd timer (or cron) runs
  `certbot renew` twice daily with a deploy-hook that reloads nginx; nginx serves an
  `/.well-known/acme-challenge/` location (webroot) so HTTP-01 renewal succeeds. Installed by
  the bootstrap script.
- **secrets bootstrap (SSM -> ephemeral `.env`).** `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` runs
  `aws ssm get-parameter --with-decryption` for the signing key + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (via the
  **instance role**, no static keys) and writes a root-only (`0600`) `.env` that compose reads,
  BEFORE `docker compose up`. The `.env` is gitignored + ephemeral (regenerated each boot, never
  committed); the script is idempotent and **aborts** if SSM is unreachable (never reuses a
  stale `.env`). Recorded in `docs/adr/0008-secrets-source-ssm.md`.
- **IAM instance role (least privilege).** Grants `ssm:GetParameter`/`GetParameters` scoped to
  the project parameter-path prefix only (e.g.
  `arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:896586841071:parameter/rebar/prod/*`) plus `kms:Decrypt` on the
  SecureString key — no wildcard. Defined in the infra IaC.
- **observability (minimal).** Both containers log via `journald`; a health probe hits `/` and
  the receiver `/review/` health path; a disk-usage alarm guards the Gerrit data volume.

## Scope
In: containers, the external Gerrit data volume DECLARATION, base `gerrit.config`,
nginx+TLS+certbot renewal, the review-bot receiver HTTP surface + hosting, SSM->.env secrets
bootstrap, the SSM IAM instance role, minimal logging/health/disk observability. Out: Gerrit
project/label/submit-req (S3); the bot identity + event wiring + the receiver's review/vote
LOGIC (S4 — S2 stands up the *service*, S4 wires its behavior); replication (S5); the data
volume's snapshot + retention policy (S7's DLM daily-snapshot policy owns it — S2 only declares
the volume).

## Files
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`, `infra/compose/gerrit.config`, `infra/nginx/*.conf`
  (incl. the `/.well-known/acme-challenge/` location and the `location /review/` upstream)
- `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` (SSM -> ephemeral `.env`), bootstrap scripts under `infra/`
  (install the certbot-renew timer, the health/disk checks)
- `src/rebar/review_bot/` (the webhook-receiver ASGI service — skeleton/health endpoint in S2;
  review+vote behavior in S4)
- `docs/adr/0007-review-bot-receiver.md`, `docs/adr/0008-secrets-source-ssm.md`
- `.gitignore` (ensure `infra/compose/.env` is ignored)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gerrit is reachable at `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/` with a valid Let's Encrypt cert.
- [ ] A Gerrit change screen / a project path containing `%2F` renders correctly (encoded-slash round-trip verified end-to-end through nginx).
- [ ] certbot renewal is automated: the timer/cron is installed + enabled, the nginx `/.well-known/acme-challenge/` location serves the HTTP-01 challenge, and `certbot renew --dry-run` succeeds and reloads nginx via the deploy-hook.
- [ ] Gerrit SSH clone works on 29418 and the admin account is bootstrapped.
- [ ] Gerrit data persists across `docker compose down/up` and an instance reboot; the data volume is `external: true` so `docker compose down -v` does not destroy it. (Snapshot + retention of this volume is owned by S7, not S2.)
- [ ] The review-bot webhook receiver (a plain HTTP service importing the review kernel as a library, NOT the stdio MCP over HTTP) is reachable through nginx at `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/review/` — a POST reaches the receiver process (not a 502); the decision is recorded in `docs/adr/0007-review-bot-receiver.md`.
- [ ] The review-bot container's signing key + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are sourced from SSM at boot by `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` into a `0600`, gitignored, ephemeral `.env` (never baked into the image/compose, never committed); the script authenticates via the instance role, is idempotent, and aborts if SSM is unreachable. The receiver's LLM review path is enabled by `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` + the `[agents]` extra alone (no `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` flag, which gates only the stdio MCP server, is set).
- [ ] The SSM IAM instance role grants `GetParameter`/`GetParameters` scoped to `parameter/rebar/prod/*` only (no wildcard), verified against the attached policy.
- [ ] Both containers log to `journald`; a health probe covers `/` and `/review/`, and a disk-usage alarm covers the Gerrit data volume.
- [ ] Gerrit runs a supported version at an EXISTING arm64 patch tag (e.g. `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1`; a bare minor tag like `3.14` is NOT used — it doesn't exist), with `heapLimit=2g` and `packedGitLimit` budgeted within it.

## Round-12 plan-review remediation (2026-06-30 — recalibrated gate BLOCK + critical advisories)

**Blocking — internal sequencing (phased build, each phase verified before the next):**
- **Phase 1 (riskiest first):** Gerrit `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (arm64) container up with the external data volume + base `gerrit.config`; reachable over plain HTTP through nginx (no TLS yet); `%2F`-encoded path round-trips. Proves the arm64 image + the `%2F`-safe proxy before anything else is layered on.
- **Phase 2:** nginx TLS via certbot (Let's Encrypt) on 443 + the `/.well-known/acme-challenge/` webroot + the twice-daily `certbot renew` timer with the nginx-reload deploy-hook; `certbot renew --dry-run` green.
- **Phase 3:** `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` (SSM->ephemeral 0600 `.env`) via the instance role + the scoped IAM policy attachment (S2's separately-named inline policy on the S1-owned role).
- **Phase 4:** the review-bot receiver container reachable through nginx at `/review/` (skeleton + health only — review/vote LOGIC is S4b).
- **Phase 5:** observability (journald, `/` + `/review/` health probes, data-volume disk alarm).

**Critical advisories folded in:**
- **`webhooks` plugin is NOT bundled in the official image (seam to S4a):** S2 stands up the *service* only. The Gerrit site's `plugins/` dir is a bind-mount/volume path so a version-matched (3.14.1) `webhooks` + `events-log` plugin jar can be dropped in; ACTUAL plugin install + wiring is **S4a**. S2 verifies only that the site supports dropping plugin jars (the plugins dir is writable + on the persistent volume). No S2 AC depends on webhooks delivering events.
- **Receiver framework dependency (was absent):** add a `reviewbot` optional extra to `pyproject.toml` = `fastapi` + `uvicorn[standard]`. The receiver container builds via `infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot` (`pip install .[agents,reviewbot]`). The stdio MCP server is untouched.
- **Dockerfile added** to Files: `infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot`.
- **`core.packedGitLimit` is OFF-HEAP (correction):** JGit's `core.packedGitLimit` is the mmap'd packfile budget, sized against TOTAL RAM, NOT inside the Java heap. Config: `container.heapLimit=2g` (on-heap) + `core.packedGitLimit` ~1g budgeted against the ~8 GB box RAM ALONGSIDE (not within) the heap, leaving headroom for the OS + the receiver container.
- **`fetch-secrets.sh` explicit SSM-leaf -> env mapping** (the 7 params S1 created): `/rebar/prod/gerrit-admin-password`->`GERRIT_ADMIN_PASSWORD`, `gerrit-ssh-host-ed25519-key`->`GERRIT_SSH_HOST_ED25519_KEY`, `github-replication-deploy-key`->`GITHUB_REPLICATION_DEPLOY_KEY`, `mcp-hmac-signing-key`->`MCP_HMAC_SIGNING_KEY`, `anthropic-api-key`->`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `alert-endpoint`->`ALERT_ENDPOINT`, `gerrit-bot-token`->`GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`. (Same contract as S1's user_data.sh; S2 sources only the subset the containers need.)
- **LLM path enablement:** the receiver's LLM review path is enabled by `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` + the `[agents]` extra ALONE — `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` (which gates only the stdio MCP server) is intentionally NOT set.
- **External volume creation step:** the Gerrit data volume is a Docker `external: true` volume backed by the `/var/gerrit` EBS mount; `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` (the boot orchestrator) runs `docker volume create --driver local --opt type=none --opt o=bind --opt device=/var/gerrit gerrit_data` (idempotent) BEFORE `docker compose up`, so a stray `docker compose down -v` cannot destroy it.
- **Port single-source:** the receiver port is one env var `REVIEW_BOT_PORT` (default 8000) in the `.env`; compose and the nginx upstream both read it (nginx via an envsubst-templated conf at boot), so the two can't drift.

**Added / updated ACs:**
- [ ] The build is phased (Phase 1 Gerrit+HTTP -> 2 TLS -> 3 secrets/IAM -> 4 receiver -> 5 observability); Phase 1 reachability is proven before TLS is layered on.
- [ ] `pyproject.toml` declares a `reviewbot` extra (fastapi + uvicorn); `infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot` builds the receiver via `.[agents,reviewbot]`.
- [ ] `gerrit.config` sets `container.heapLimit=2g` (on-heap) and `core.packedGitLimit` as a SEPARATE off-heap budget (~1g) sized against box RAM, NOT inside the heap.
- [ ] `fetch-secrets.sh` maps the enumerated `/rebar/prod/*` leaves to the documented env-var names, writes a 0600 ephemeral `.env`, authenticates via the instance role, is idempotent, and aborts if SSM is unreachable.
- [ ] The Gerrit data volume is created as an `external` bind volume on `/var/gerrit` by `compose-up.sh` before `docker compose up`; `docker compose down -v` does not destroy it.
- [ ] The receiver port is single-sourced (`REVIEW_BOT_PORT`) and consumed by both compose and the nginx `/review/` upstream.
- [ ] The Gerrit site `plugins/` dir is on the persistent volume + writable (so S4a can drop version-matched webhooks/events-log jars); no S2 AC depends on event delivery.

file_impact.reason: external volume create + compose up boot orchestrator
certbot renew timer + nginx reload hook
S2 scoped inline policy on S1 instance role

compiled_state.title: infra: S2 — Deploy Gerrit + rebar MCP (docker-compose) behind nginx + TLS
